title: For Loops
description: Execute code repeatedly without copying and pasting.

slides.title: The Repetition Problem 🔁
slides.content:
## Automating Work

If you want to print the word "Hello" 5 times, you could write `println("Hello")` on 5 separate lines.

But what if you need to print it 10,000 times? That's what Loops are for.

slides.title: The Basic For Loop 🚀
slides.content:
The `for` loop in Kotlin allows you to iterate (step through) a range of values.

`for (i in 1..5)` means "Create a temporary variable `i`. Set it to 1, run the block. Then set it to 2, run the block... until you hit 5."

quizData.question: How many times will a `for (i in 1..3)` loop execute its code block?
quizData.explanation: Because `1..3` includes 1, 2, and 3, the block runs precisely 3 times.

slides.title: Stepping and Reversing 🛤️
slides.content:
By default, a loop counts up by 1 (+1). 

We can modify this behavior. Use `step` to skip numbers, and `downTo` to count backward!

quizData.question: Which keyword must you use to create a range that counts backwards?
quizData.explanation: You cannot write `5..1`. You must explicitly tell Kotlin to count backwards using `5 downTo 1`.
